mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate spikes above 6.0% within a single quarter, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts signals a synchronized demand shock that overwhelms corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of observed episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, elevated inflation readings force the market to price in higher terminal rates, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise concurrently, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive equity returns over a 20-day forward window.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate positively with 20-day SPX returns across any 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints trigger cost-push inflation that erodes corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO breaches the 95.0 threshold, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in payroll employment and a decline in the unemployment rate signals a robust labor market that fuels wage-driven inflation, prompting equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit contraction regime, a sustained decline in residential housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader economic slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of credit contraction episodes, or the forward return distribution shows no significant left skew during triggered periods.
